content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

December 11, 1989

Cathy Wilcox-Barnes
City Clerk
317 Broad Street
Nevada City, CA 95959

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
    Our File No. I-89-658

Dear Ms. Wilcox-Barnes:

    This is in response to your letter of November 14, 1989,
requesting advice concerning the conflict-of-interest provisions
of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Since the advice you
requested is of a general nature and does not name a specific
party, we consider your request to be for informal assistance.
Consequently, we can only provide the following informal
guidelines with respect to your questions.2

QUESTION

    You have inquired whether an employee of the City of Nevada
may serve as an elected member of the City Council without creating a conflict of interest under the Act.

CONCLUSION

    The Act does not prohibit a city employee from seeking election to the position of councilmember for the City of Nevada City.
However, because other legal provisions could impact on the
employee's situation, you should contact your city attorney or
county counsel as to those matters, as discussed in this letter.

___________
1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section
83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

Our File No. I-89-658
Page 2


FACTS

An employee of the City of Nevada City ("the employee")
intends to be a candidate for the city council of that city.

ANALYSIS

Every employee of a local government agency is considered a
"public official" for purposes of the Act. (Section 82048.)
Under the facts in this instance, by definition this includes the
employee's present employment with the city and would also include
his contemplated position as an elected member of the City of
Nevada City city council. Section 87100 prohibits any public of-
ficial from making, participating in, or using his official posi-
tion to influence a governmental decision in which he knows or has
reason to know he has a financial interest.

Although public officials must generally refrain from
participating in decisions which will have a material financial
effect on sources of income to them (Section 87103(c)), salary
from a federal, state, or local government agency is not "income"
under the Act. (Section 82030(b)(2).) Thus, neither the
employee's salary as a county employee nor any salary received as
a council member would, in themselves, give rise to a conflict of
interest under Section 87103(c). Therefore, the Act would not
prevent him from participating in governmental decisions upon
election to the city council solely because of his city employ-
ment.

Because the advice provided by the Fair Political Practices
Commission is limited to the provisions of the Act, other legal
provisions, such as Government Code Section 1090, the general
doctrine of incompatible offices, or any local ordinances which
may have some bearing on the situation are beyond the scope of
this letter. You or the employee should contact the city attorney
and county counsel for advice as to these matters.

I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you
requested. If you have any further questions regarding this mat-
ter, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Joseph Garcia
Counsel, Legal Division

KED:JG:aa

CITY OF NEVADA CITY
CALIFORNIA
November 14, 1989

California Fair Political Practices Commission
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
Attn: Lynne Martin
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807


RE: Question on Eligibility to Run for City Council Position


Dear Lynne:

I have been asked by an employee of the City of Nevada City if he can run for a seat on the City Council. He resides within the City Limits and is a registered voter. Nevada City is a General Law City and the Council seat is a volunteer (unpaid) position.

I was unable to answer his question and thought that you might be able to assist me.

If you need any further information from me, don't hesitate to contact me. I appreciate any assistance you can give me on this matter.


Sincerely,

Cathy Wilcox-Barnes
City Clerk


CITY HALL • 317 BROAD STREET • NEVADA CITY, CA 95959 • (916) 265-2496

November 20, 1989


Cathy Wilcox-Barnes
City Clerk, City of Nevada City
City Hall, 317 Broad Street
Nevada City, CA 95959

Re: Letter No. 89-658

Dear Ms. Wilcox-Barnes:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on November 16, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact Joe Garcia an attorney in the Legal Division,
directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to
information needed. If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can. (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

KED:plh


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660
